skill.name: api-documenter
skill.description: Master API documentation with OpenAPI 3.1, AI-powered tools, and modern developer experience practices. Create interactive docs, generate SDKs, and build comprehensive developer portals. Use PROACTIVELY for API documentation or developer portal creation.
skill.summary: Master API documentation with OpenAPI 3.1, AI-powered tools, and modern developer experience practic...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and network reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found only Markdown guidance about API documentation, developer portals, analytics, and design practices, with no executable code or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False-Positive Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are prose inside a documentation skill. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, weak hashing, encryption code, scripts, or executable implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a Markdown instruction document, not source code. The flagged lines discuss API documentation concepts and contain no cryptographic calls or unsafe algorithm recommendations.

low_findings.title: False-Positive Network Reconnaissance Pattern Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines mention documentation analytics, developer success, and clarity. No evidence found of scanning tools, host enumeration, probing commands, or network reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matches occur in non-executable prose and do not reference reconnaissance utilities or network targets. The semantic context is developer documentation quality, not scanning.

content.value_statement: API teams often struggle to keep specifications, examples, and portals clear and current. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through OpenAPI documentation, SDK guidance, and developer portal planning.
actual_capabilities: Plans and writes OpenAPI 3.1 and AsyncAPI documentation workflows.
Improves API reference structure, examples, authentication sections, and error documentation.
Guides developer portal information architecture and onboarding content.
Recommends SDK documentation and code example generation practices.
Defines documentation validation, testing, versioning, and maintenance approaches.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated generators.
Requires the user to provide API details, schemas, or existing documentation.
Cannot verify live endpoints unless the host AI tool has separate access.
Does not replace legal, compliance, or security review of public API terms.

use_cases.title: Launch Public API Docs
use_cases.description: Create a structured API reference with authentication, examples, errors, and onboarding guidance.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Specifications
use_cases.description: Review OpenAPI or AsyncAPI content for missing examples, unclear schemas, and versioning gaps.

use_cases.title: Plan SDK Documentation
use_cases.description: Design language-specific guides, package installation notes, and generated client usage examples.

prompt_templates.title: Create an API Documentation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-documenter skill to create a documentation plan for my API. Include audience, required pages, examples, authentication, errors, and maintenance tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Review an OpenAPI Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-documenter skill to review this OpenAPI specification. Identify missing descriptions, weak examples, unclear schemas, authentication gaps, and versioning concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Developer Portal
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-documenter skill to design a developer portal for multiple APIs. Include navigation, search, onboarding, API explorer behavior, SDK docs, and feedback loops.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Documentation Automation Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the api-documenter skill to define a docs-as-code workflow. Include validation, example testing, CI checks, changelog handling, localization, and release publishing.

output_examples.input: Create documentation for a payments API.
output_examples.output: A documentation outline covering authentication, payment creation, refunds, webhooks, error handling, rate limits, and quickstart steps.

output_examples.input: Review this API reference for developer experience.
output: Findings grouped by navigation, endpoint clarity, schema completeness, examples, authentication, and troubleshooting.
Recommended changes for clearer onboarding and fewer support requests.

output_examples.input: Plan SDK docs for Python and JavaScript clients.
output_examples.output: A guide structure with installation, configuration, common workflows, retries, errors, versioning notes, and release maintenance guidance.

best_practices: Provide real API schemas, authentication details, and example requests before asking for documentation.
Ask for validation criteria so examples, schemas, and error responses stay testable.
Review generated public docs with product, support, security, and legal stakeholders.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent endpoint behavior without source specifications.
Do not publish generated docs before testing examples against the real API.
Do not use it for backend implementation tasks that have no documentation scope.

faq.question: What documentation standards does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on OpenAPI 3.1, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema documentation, webhook docs, and developer portal content.

faq.question: Can it generate SDKs directly?
faq.answer: It can guide SDK generation strategy and documentation, but it does not include executable generator scripts.

faq.question: Does it support interactive API documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidance for API explorers, try-it-now flows, examples, authentication handling, and portal structure.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only Markdown guidance and no executable code, network calls, or prompt injection.

faq.question: What input should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide endpoint lists, schemas, authentication rules, error formats, examples, target users, and existing documentation links.

faq.question: Can it help maintain documentation over time?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers docs-as-code workflows, validation, example testing, versioning, changelogs, and deprecation notices.
